user.title: Connect to Mamma Mi Radio
user.description: Point Home Assistant at your Mamma Mi Radio add-on.

data_description.host: The add-on's address. On Home Assistant OS keep the default; on Docker use the container name (for example mammamiradio).
data_description.admin_token: Needed only when Home Assistant reaches the add-on from outside its trusted network (a remote or Docker install). On Home Assistant OS the controls work without it.

reconfigure.title: Update Mamma Mi Radio
reconfigure.description: Update the station address or admin token. Home Assistant will reload the integration after saving.

data_description.host: The add-on's address. On Home Assistant OS keep the default; on Docker use the container name (for example mammamiradio).
data_description.admin_token: Use the same value as the add-on's admin_token option. Leave blank when the add-on trusts the Supervisor network.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the station. Check the host and port, give the add-on a few seconds to finish starting, then try again.
error.unknown: Something unexpected happened. Make sure the add-on is running, then try again.

abort.already_configured: This station is already set up.

admin_token_rejected.message: The station did not accept that. Set the add-on's admin token in the integration reconfigure screen, then try again.

control_failed.message: The station could not do that just now. Give it a few seconds and try again.

station_unreachable_control.message: Could not reach the station. Check the add-on is running, then try again.

admin_token_rejected.title: Mamma Mi Radio admin token was rejected
admin_token_rejected.description: Home Assistant can see the station, but the station rejected a play, stop, or next command. Reconfigure the Mamma Mi Radio integration and enter the same admin token used by the add-on, then try the control again.

station_unreachable.title: Mamma Mi Radio is unreachable
station_unreachable.description: Home Assistant has missed several updates from the station. Check that the Mamma Mi Radio add-on is running and that the integration host and port are correct. You can reconfigure the integration without deleting the entity.

legacy_media_player_push_conflict.title: Mamma Mi Radio has a legacy media player conflict
legacy_media_player_push_conflict.description: A REST-pushed media_player.mammamiradio state already exists while the HACS integration is trying to own the same entity. In the add-on Configuration tab, turn off On-air media player push. The sensors keep working, and the integration keeps the controllable media player. After turning it off, reload the Mamma Mi Radio integration to clear this notice.
